title: Benzoin urophyllum

Traditionally, Benzoin urophyllum has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Benzoin urophyllum is often prepared into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
